claim: Agent Teams involve multiple agents communicating on a shared task list in real time, unlike subagents which work in isolation and report back with only a summary.
actionable: Use Agent Teams when you need cross-team coordination (e.g., backend agent notifies frontend agent of API changes); use subagents for isolated research tasks.
source_title: Claude Code's Agent Teams Are Insane

claim: Agent Teams are 2-4x more token-expensive than single agents or subagents due to maintaining task lists and coordination overhead, but necessary for complex implementation work.
actionable: Budget for higher token usage when using Agent Teams; reserve them for tasks where coordination value justifies the cost.
source_title: Claude Code's Agent Teams Are Insane

claim: Agent Teams can struggle with parallelization: agents may start work before receiving status from dependent agents, leading to wasted effort and the need for rework.
actionable: Use 'contract first spawning' to establish dependencies (e.g., database schema first, then backend) before spinning up all agents in parallel.
source_title: Claude Code's Agent Teams Are Insane

claim: Without explicit instructions, Claude Code may hallucinate how to form agent teams or mishandle T-Mux terminals, so a dedicated skill/prompt with team formation logic improves reliability.
actionable: Build a custom skill with instructions for Claude Code on optimal team composition, terminal management, and dependency ordering for your domain.
source_title: Claude Code's Agent Teams Are Insane

claim: Use subagents for research (codebase analysis, web search) and Agent Teams for implementation; chain them as: research → plan → team-based build.
actionable: Start coding tasks with subagent research to understand the scope, create a plan, then spawn an Agent Team with explicit coordination instructions for implementation.
source_title: Claude Code's Agent Teams Are Insane

claim: Anthropic built a C compiler with 16 agents in Agent Teams, requiring only $20k in API costs but producing hundreds of thousands of lines of code.
actionable: Consider Agent Teams for large-scale code generation tasks; the cost-per-line-of-code can be remarkably efficient despite high token usage.
source_title: Claude Code's Agent Teams Are Insane
